source_issue.title: Add deprecation policy or guide to documentation

summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on creating clear deprecation policies and guidelines for Volto developers. Currently, Volto documentation lacks a dedicated guide for deprecating components, which is essential for maintaining clarity during development transitions.

## What needs to be done
We need to establish a deprecation policy and how-to guide for Volto. Possible approaches include:
- Adding a "Deprecation policy" reference page.
- Incorporating a "How to deprecate" section with practical examples based on existing Plone 6 guidelines.
 
### Note
The discussion is still ongoing about the best structure for these documents. Internal team conversation is necessary before any substantial work begins.

## Helpful background
In a programming context, "deprecation" refers to marking a feature as outdated while allowing developers time to transition away from it. Clear guidelines help prevent confusion during updates.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with current [Volto documentation](https://6.docs.plone.org/volto/).
2. Look at the existing [Version policy](https://6.docs.plone.org/volto/contributing/version-policy.html) and [Upgrade Guide](https://6.docs.plone.org/volto/upgrade-guide/index.html).
3. Monitor any updates in the ongoing conversation in this GitHub issue for direction on how to contribute.
```
